text: The surgeon initiates the procedure using a medium approach, identifying and protecting the superior hypogastric plexus. Central lymph nodes surrounding the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) and inferior mesenteric vein (IMV) are excised.

The surgeon isolates and cuts the left colic artery at its origin while retaining the superior rectal artery. The hypogastric nerves are identified and protected. The mesocolon is dissected from median to lateral along the left toe line, with the left ureter identified and protected.
The surgeon ligates and cuts the root of the inferior mesenteric vein (IMV), followed by granular dissection of the mesocolon extending to the upper border of the pancreas.

The surgeon mobilizes the sigmoid colon laterally, distally towards the rectum, and proximally towards the descending colon. The splenic flexure is also mobilized, confirming complete mobilization of the sigmoid and descending colon.
The surgeon removes the specimen through a small incision, followed by extracorporeal anastomosis. The final pathology reveals stage IIIb colon cancer.
